title: PayPal Q1 2026 Earnings Slides: Beats Estimates But Guidance Weighs On Stock
text: PayPal reported Q1 2026 revenue of $8.35 billion, beating analyst estimates while guidance weighed on the stock. The company achieved non-GAAP EPS of $1.34, surpassing estimates of $1.27. Total payment volume reached $463.96 billion with 11% year-over-year growth on a spot basis and 8% on a foreign exchange-neutral basis. However, for Q2 2026, PayPal projects a high-single-digit decline in non-GAAP EPS of approximately -9% compared to $1.40 in Q2 2025. Full-year 2026 guidance remains for non-GAAP EPS ranging from slightly down to slightly positive. The company expects at least $6 billion in adjusted free cash flow and plans to continue share repurchase programs. A strategic reorganization into three distinct market opportunities requires near-term investments that will impact margins. The presentation revealed a comprehensive strategic overhaul aimed at generating at least $1.5 billion in gross run-rate savings.
